title: Solar MPPT Charger (SEPIC)
description: A SEPIC converter charging a 12 V lead-acid or LiFePO4 battery bank at up to 28.8 W from a solar panel whose open-circuit voltage varies between 6 V and 18 V depending on irradiance.
design_notes: The SEPIC topology is uniquely suited to MPPT solar charging because the output voltage (14.4 V battery charge voltage) can be both above and below the panel voltage, allowing operation from a single low-cell-count panel across its full MPP voltage range (typically 70–80 % of Voc). Unlike a buck-boost, the SEPIC maintains non-inverted polarity and presents a continuous input current, which is gentler on the panel's output impedance. The coupling capacitor between the two inductors is a 10 µF film or large-value X7R ceramic; avoid electrolytic here as ripple current is high. Full MPPT requires a perturb-and-observe or incremental-conductance outer loop above this power-stage model — that logic runs in a microcontroller measuring Vpanel and Ipanel.
source: TI SLVA061 — SEPIC Converter Design; Erickson & Maksimovic §11.5
